# fortytwo

fortytwo is a local-first personal-assistant spine for the agents and tools you already use.
It wraps an existing harness with memory, a safety gate, channels, approvals, and audit.
The answer is 42. The hard part is knowing the right question.

## Install
- npm: `npm install @justfortytwo/installer` then `npx create-fortytwo init`
  (init installs the engine packages it needs, then scaffolds the persona)
- Claude Code: `/plugin marketplace add justfortytwo/marketplace` then `/plugin install fortytwo@fortytwo`

## Packages (published to npm under @justfortytwo)
- gate — PreToolUse safety gate: allow / defer / deny, fail-closed
- memory — semantic-memory MCP server (SQLite + sqlite-vec vectors + FTS5)
- salience — model-driven salience extraction for memory enrichment
- telegram — Telegram channel adapter / pairing bridge
- persona — CLAUDE.md + context templates (scaffolded per-user, never shipped filled)
- installer — create-fortytwo / fortytwo: the install + lifecycle CLI
- marketplace — the Claude Code plugin marketplace (installed from GitHub, not npm)

## Links
- GitHub: https://github.com/justfortytwo
- License: MIT
